programmes.show_description: Can forensic science solve the murder of Melissa Lee, which baffled Washington detectives in 1993? S5, Ep19
A man comes to the rescue of his disabled mother while her daughter allegedly reneges on payback of a loan for her and her four children. S24, Ep11
While crossing the street in a motorised wheelchair, a woman is struck by an uninsured vehicle and suffers severe road rash. S24, Ep12
The Judge openly admits she dislikes a married man being sued by his ex-girlfriend; she says he went back to his wife and took her truck with him. S24, Ep13
An attorney claims he succeeded in having a difficult tenant evicted for his landlord client; he sues for the alleged remainder of his fees. S22, Ep134
A man claims a large Australian Shepherd pushed through a fence gap to bite his dog's paw; his neighbours say it was impossible. S22, Ep135
The Judge is incredulous when a man pre-emptively educates himself about how to prevent his Pit Bull from maiming him. S22, Ep136
All hell breaks loose when a woman's handyman starts dating one of her tenants; she files a restraining order and sues him for alleged vandalism. S22, Ep137
After a young man is rescued by a family friend, he makes a deal to move in and pay her back; she says he was like a son but sues him anyway. S22, Ep138
An evening of tequila drinking evolves into a reckless car ride that ends with an alleged collision. S22, Ep139
Squirrels in an attic, trash in the basement and tenant identity confusion come into play when a woman sues her landlord. S22, Ep140
A celebrity photographer is sued by his ex-lover for allegedly making unauthorized charges on her credit card; he countersues for damages from a dog bite. S16, Ep53
A custody battle over a young girl gets out of control. Her father John Blattner is shot dead. His ex-wife Jennifer and her new husband Kevin Bowen both plead guilty. S3, Ep6
Series presented by Nick Knowles which shows the split-second moments when everyday events are transformed into disasters and meets the people who lived to tell the tale. S2, Ep16
Series presented by Nick Knowles which shows the split-second moments when everyday events are transformed into disasters and meets the people who lived to tell the tale. S2, Ep17
Innovative, value-for-money products brought directly to you at home. Shop 'til you drop whilst avoiding the hustle and bustle of the High Street.
A landscaper is accused of vandalizing a woman's yard after a dispute over finances. S22, Ep141
A woman sues her teenage daughter and her boyfriend for allegedly totalling a car she was allowing her to borrow. S22, Ep142
A woman sues her estranged son-in-law for money he received for housing while out of the country; she believes it should go to his wife and child. S22, Ep143
A teenager's car is allegedly beaten with a bat when he admits to going out of his way to meet up with friends arguing over a girl. S22, Ep144
When a man feels stiffed after a car deal, he is accused of taking out his frustration on a tenant by illegally evicting him. S22, Ep145
A vehicle takes a severe 'knifing' when a woman vandalises her baby daddy's ex-girlfriend's vehicle. S24, Ep14
The Judge cites a study naming the top 'biting' dogs, as she mediates a case involving the mauling death of a Terrier by a Pit Bull mix. S24, Ep15
A woman sues her former hairdresser for an unpaid loan to hire a divorce attorney; she claims she was paying off the loan by styling her hair for free. S16, Ep2
An alleged threat to report a dirt bike as stolen leads to a woman returning the bike to its owner despite being on time in her payments; she sues him for the return of payments she made. S16, Ep3
A woman is suing her sister for damages to an SUV that was in her name; her sister says she repossessed the vehicle two years ago. S16, Ep4
While serving in Iraq, a man meets a woman on Facebook whom he begins dating when he returns home. Both single parents, they end up fighting in court over money. S16, Ep5
Alleged physical abuse of an autistic minor, $15,000 in back child support and a DNA test to prove paternity come into play when a woman sues her ex-boyfriend's fiancé. S16, Ep6
Elementary school teachers fight in front of their students then sue each other over 52 dollars worth of missing textbooks and alleged slander. S16, Ep7
After an unpleasant break-up, a malicious e-mail is allegedly sent to a woman's entire company when she insists that her ex-boyfriend pay her back for a loan to fix his car. S16, Ep8
A teenager confesses that she stole money from her mother to give to her 16-year-old boyfriend; the boy's mother admits to being complicit in accepting part of the $2,400 received. S16, Ep9
A woman claims she sent racy pictures of herself in a negligee with another man to her ex-boyfriend and that's why she's being sued for his impounded car which she totalled. S16, Ep10
Inspection of a man's handwriting, defamation of character and an alleged assault come into play when ex-friends fight over repossession of a truck. S16, Ep11
A man who fathered four children while disabled sues his landlord for allegedly poor living conditions, retaliation and harassment. S24, Ep16
A teenager accuses her 32-year-old ex-boyfriend of stealing her dog; Judge Judy threatens to take away his plane ticket home if he doesn't return it to her. S24, Ep17
When a woman is accused of slamming her car door into a parked vehicle, she resorts to claiming that the process server touched her inappropriately. S24, Ep18
The FBI probes bombings and bank robberies attributed to a white supremacist group; the Phineas Priesthood, in Spokane, Washington. S5, Ep7
In Bartow, Florida four bodies are found slaughtered in a factory. It's the largest mass murder in county history. Each victim is connected to the factory and the family business. S5, Ep8
In September 1990, the body of twenty-two year old college student, Linda Yalem, is discovered off a bike path in a suburb of Buffalo, New York. S5, Ep9
In the US, Brian Cohee Jr., 19, murdered a local man, hid body parts, and later confessed. Obsessed with violence, he was sentenced to life in prison. S1, Ep5
US model Kelsey Turner's 'Sugar Daddy' scheme turns deadly when she and boyfriend, Jon, murder Dr Burchard after financial betrayal and false accusations. S1, Ep6
In the US, Ali Abulaban, a former airman, abused Ana, then killed her and her friend after she left him. He was convicted of two counts of first-degree murder. S1, Ep7

An emergency room visit for an 8-year-old and the cost of psychiatric care for an alleged attention deficit disorder are disputed by divorcees. S16, Ep54
When Danny Pelosi starts having an affair with socialite Generosa Ammon he thinks he's won the lottery. Months later Generosa's husband Ted is found bludgeoned to death. S4, Ep1

Two Pit Bull Mastiffs allegedly drag their owner across the street in order to viciously attack another dog. S22, Ep146
A generous boss loans his employees over $3,000 so they won't lose their home; when they fail to pay him back, he's forced to sue them. S22, Ep147
A woman is accused of making outrageous statements to authorities when she tries to regain access to her illegally sublet apartment. S22, Ep148
A woman is forced to evict her brother in order to sell her deceased father's home; he takes issue with the lawyer he hired to help him. S22, Ep149
A boy's pet turtle, calls to the police and a controversial electric bill come into play when ex-fiancée's call it quits. S22, Ep150
After children deny involvement in vandalising a neighbour's car, Judge Judy imparts an invaluable lesson. S24, Ep19
A woman accused of stealing thousands from her cousin tries to clear her name by passing a polygraph test. S24, Ep20
A home-based Internet business, a divorce, and an ex-employee caught in the middle come into play when ex-roommates fight over rent. S16, Ep12
Claiming she made over $7,000 in car payments, a young woman sues her ex-stepfather after he repossesses her vehicle. S16, Ep13
A senior citizen is accused of assaulting another senior at a Ladies' Auxiliary VFW meeting and is escorted out of her hotel room by police. She sues for damages. S16, Ep14
After meeting a mechanic at a bowling alley, a woman ends up suing him for allegedly selling her broken-down BMW without her permission; he claims he was forced to get rid of it. S16, Ep15
A mother's allegedly inappropriate behaviour with her daughter's fiancé, drinking problems and damage caused by Chihuahuas come into play in a fight over fishing poles. S16, Ep16
After ruining her credit, a woman demands that her boyfriend's adult son return the car she co-signed for him or give her money to pay it off. S16, Ep17
A woman who claims her ex-fiancé wasn't ready to get married sues him for alleged loans to buy a motorcycle, make a mortgage payment, and pay his tuition. S16, Ep18
A man is accused of making homophobic comments and being rude to a fellow roommate; he sues his ex-roommate for the return of rent and a security deposit. S16, Ep19
A $5,000 marijuana drug deal gone wrong is disputed by ex-roommates who fight over alleged damages as a result of a police raid. S16, Ep20
After eight weeks of work, a young man says he was injured on the job and sues his boss for $5,000 for an alleged wrongful termination and a workman's comp claim. S16, Ep21
The parents of a teenager are told that his ex-lover gave birth; she sends them pictures of a live newborn but then says she miscarried at nine months. S24, Ep21
A woman has the nerve to sue for a false arrest after trading a car which she still owed over 10K on; the new owners claim they were never told. S24, Ep22
A man is accused of lying to his landlord about his dog being a service animal; she says his pet destroyed her property. S24, Ep23
Prostitutes in Spokane, Washington, are being hunted down and discarded in remote wooded areas by a vicious serial killer. On each victim's head, police find his macabre calling card. S5, Ep10
A serial rapist preys on elderly women living in isolated Southern Ontario. Who is this monster that continues to elude Colchester police? S5, Ep11
Thomas Downey is murdered in Bath. DNA and CCTV lead to Darrell Richards being jailed in 2015. S1, Ep3
Dawn Walker's hope for a happy marriage ended on her wedding night when Thomas Nutt murdered her in West Yorkshire. He hid her body in a suitcase, then faked a search. S3, Ep8
Dr Peter Coshan, a retired teacher in Edinburgh, vanishes after falling victim to a fraudster's charms. S1, Ep1
